title: 2 Pigs Escaped in Woodbridge CT on the Same Day — 1 Is Still Missing
script: Pigs on the loose! On June 10, police in Woodbridge, Connecticut got 2 calls about 2 separate pigs wandering loose in the same town on the same day. The first call came in at 5:45 a.m. — a pig spotted in a yard on Old Quarry Road. A second call came in from Acorn Hill Road, where neighbors said that pig had been roaming for at least 2 days. Woodbridge Regional Animal Control teamed up with officers from Naugatuck and Prospect, plus a local vet, to track them both down. They caught one pig. The other is still at large. Police are now asking residents to watch for the missing swine and report sightings while investigators try to identify the owners — or owner. Is this 1 escape artist owner, or did 2 strangers just happen to lose their pigs in Woodbridge on the same morning?
